phantoms.why: Checkpoint file is created, but the saved state is placeholder-only and hardcodes version 2.3.0 instead of capturing live session state.
Command prints a restore summary, but it does not restore memory/context into runtime systems and is hardwired to version 2.3.0 semantics.
Debate logic works in isolation, but no production plan-council wiring was found.
HUD integration works, but the claimed session-end reliability evidence/report card is missing from TypeScript runtime flow.
Governance exists, but current defaults/behavior do not match the phase-1 advisory-first claim and collusion soft-block behavior is incomplete.
tier-status CLI renders snapshot JSON, but the underlying TS memory store has no real Auto/Micro/Ship routing.
Freshness monitoring exists, but /pause does not persist freshness state as claimed.
Integration tests exist, but they do not exercise the full claimed cross-feature behaviors (real CMMS, real pause/continue flow, retry demotion).
The module persists files labeled as ship-tier, but there is no real TS ship-tier memory backend.
Router selection works, but hard-subtask escalation cost tracking is not integrated into router behavior.
Traceability helpers exist, but actual git/phase-gate integration is absent.
On-demand reports are generated, but no web-source pipeline or effective CLI token-budget enforcement exists.
Escalation helper exists, but it is not wired into the main task-routing path.
Daemon helpers exist in Python, but the TS CLI exposes no --daemon or --stop behavior.
Validator checks structure only; it does not build/test generated projects as the anti-distillation claim requires.
Soft-block mode exists, but collusion-specific blocking still requires hard-block mode.
AOS integration test is mostly synthetic and harness-runner uses fake runners rather than actual orchestration.
TypeScript memory store is monolithic; no explicit Auto/Micro/Ship tier routing exists.
Adapters exist and test cleanly, but they only expose health checks/config generation and are not complete TS provider execution surfaces.
Harness runner schema exists, but real layer execution is replaced by createPassingRunner/createFailingRunner stubs.
